provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the National Youth Council (Regulations), 2017.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to the operation of the Youth Council of Mainland Tanzania.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires: “ Act ” means the National Youth Council Act; [Cap. 441] “ controlling body “ means an authorized person dealing with youth affairs; " Council " means the Youth Council of Tanzania established under section 4(1) of the Act ; “ fees ” means the prescribed registration fee as may be determined by the Council from time to time upon approval of the Minister ; “ learning institution ” means any registered institution that is offering any course of study leading to the award of a certificate to doctorate education; “ local authority ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the local government authority Act ; [Cap. 287] " Minister " means the Minister responsible for Youth Affairs; “ Youth ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; " Organization " means a youth organization or group registered under these Regulations; Part II – Management of Youth Organization (a) – Registration

provisions.title: Registration of Youth Organization
provisions.content: (1) A Youth Organization shall not operate without being registered by the Council . (2) The Council shall keep and maintain a register of youth organizations, which shall be open to the public. (3) An application for registration under this Regulation shall be made in the prescribed form set out in the Schedule to these Regulations. (4) The application shall be accompanied by a nonrefundable fee as the Council may determine with the approval of the Minister . (5) The certificate of issued registration under this regulation shall be valid for a period of three years and may be renewed upon the payment of fees . (6) Subject to sub regulation (3) , an application shall be accompanied by— (a) a certificate of registration issued by a competent Authority under any other written laws; (b) a copy of the constitution and rules of the organization; (c) memorandum showing the name, address and designation of the controlling body responsible for the affairs of the organization; (d) names, contact details and address of persons authorised to accept service of any proceedings, notices or other correspondence on behalf of the organization; (e) payment of fees ; and (f) Letter of recommendation from a Ward or Village Executive Officer. (7) Upon receipt of an application made under this regulation the Council shall, in determining the application, consider: (a) whether the organization is registered under relevant laws; (b) the good faith of the officers and members of the organization vis a vis the objectives and goals of the organization; (c) whether or not the affairs of the organization are properly managed (d) the proposed objectives and aims of the organisation vis a vis the National Youth Development Policy; (e) the proposed sector of operation; (f) the feasibility study of the proposed programmes, projects or activities anticipated by the organization; (g) whether there is a provision on the right of youth and youth with disability to participate in activities that affect them and in economic, social and political development of the country; and (h) Any other factor as the Council may deem necessary. (8) Where conditions stipulated under this regulation are met, the Council shall register organization. (9) A certificate of registration issued under this regulation shall be as set out in the Third Schedule to these Regulations. (10) Every registered oorganization shall display the certificate of registration at a conspicuous place at its principal registered office. (b) – Accounts and audit

provisions.title: Accounts and audit
provisions.content: (1) A registered organization shall, submit to the council (a) not later than six months after the end of it’s financial year, a report of its activities and audited financial statements of the previous financial year; and (b) Estimates of income and expenditure for the coming year before the commencement of a new financial year, and not later than one month after commencement of such year. (2) The Council shall consider the reports and estimates under this regulation to satisfy itself whether they are in the best interest of the respective oorganization. (3) Where the Council is of the opinion that a submitted report and budget is against the best interests of an oorganization, it shall advise the controlling body to determine the measures to be taken in accordance with these Regulations and other relevant laws.

provisions.title: Failure to submit
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall, within three weeks after expiration of the prescribed reporting period, summon an organization that fails to submit reports and budgets to ascertain: (a) the reason for not submitting the reports and budgets; or (b) The reason for the delay. (2) Where the Council is not satisfied with the ground of failure to comply with regulation 5 , it may: (a) direct the organization to submit the relevant report or budget within the period the Council may specify; (b) suspend the organization and subject it to disciplinary measures; or (c) De-register the organisation and the organization shall cease forthwith to carry any of its activities. (c) – Monitoring and funding

provisions.title: Failure to abide Constitution
provisions.content: Where the Council is satisfied that a organization is not abiding by its Constitution, it may: (a) suspend the organization and subject it to disciplinary measures; or (b) de-register such organization and the organisation shall cease forthwith to carry any of its activities.

provisions.title: Organization funding
provisions.content: (1) A registered organization may be entitled to fund raise or receive donations from stakeholders for better carrying out its functions. (2) A registered organization shall not receive any donation without prior notification to the Council in writing. (3) Upon the receipt of the notification in sub regulation (2) , the Council may advise a registered organization with regard to policy issues before accepting a donation. (4) A donor or a person who wishes to make a donation to an organization shall: (a) satisfy itself of the status of registration of such organization; and (b) inform the council of the details of the donation. Part III – Youth Council Secretariat

provisions.title: National Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: The National Youth Council Secretariat shall be an organ of the National Youth Council for the purpose of assisting it in the exercise of its functions.

provisions.title: Composition of the National Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: The National Youth Council Secretariat shall be composed of heads of: (a) Administration and Human Resource Department; (b) Finance and Planning Department; (c) Education and Advocacy Department; (d) Leagal Services Unit; and (e) any other Department as the Council may determine.

provisions.title: Functions of National Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: The National Youth Council Secretariat shall be the: (a) chief executor of the day to day activities of the Council ; announced (b) perform any other functions as may be directed by the Council General Assembly.

provisions.title: Regional Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: There is established a Regional Youth Council Secretariat for the purposes of assisting the Regional Youth Council in exercising of its functions.

provisions.title: Composition of Regional Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: The Regional Youth Council Secretariat shall be composed of five members as follows: (a) a Secretary who shall be a youth appointed by the Regional Administrative Secretary; (b) a youth representing learning institutions to be nominated by a meeting of representatives of all learning institutions or student leadership in that particular Region. (c) one youth with disability representing organizations of persons with disabilities within that particular Region; and (d) two other members to be elected by the Regional Youth Council .

provisions.title: Functions of the Regional Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: The Regional Youth Council Secretariat shall perform the following functions: (a) coordinating community events pertaining to youth issues at Regional level; (b) furnishing the Regional Youth Council with reports on youth matters; (c) developing and maintain relationships with individuals and organizations addressing issues of concern to youth at Regional level; (d) promoting initiatives to support the well-being of youth at Regional level; (e) providing network opportunities for youth at Regional level; (f) coordinating the Region Youth Assembly, maintaining the registry of individuals participating in the General Assembly, and overseeing its meetings; and (g) informing the Regional Youth Council of any matter that may affect youth development.

provisions.title: District Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: A District Youth Council secretariat shall be an organ of the District Youth Council for the purpose of assisting it in exercising of its functions.

provisions.title: Composition of a District Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: A District Youth Council Secretariat shall be composed of five members as follows: (a) the Secretary who shall be a youth appointed by the District Administrative Secretary; (b) representative from learning institutions nominated by a meeting of Youth representatives from all learning institutions or student leadership in that particular District; (c) one youth with disability nominated representing organizations of persons with disabilities within that particular District; and (d) Two other members to be elected by the District Youth Council .

provisions.title: Function of District Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: A District Youth Council Secretariat shall perform the following functions— (a) coordinate community events pertaining to youth issues at district level; (b) furnishing District Youth Council with reports on youth matters; (c) developing and maintaining relationships with a reports on youth matters; (d) addressing issues of concern to youth at district level; (e) providing network opportunities for youth at District levels; (f) promoting initiatives to support the well-being of youth at district level; (g) coordinating the District Youth Assembly, maintain the registry of individuals participating in the General Assembly, maintaining the registry of individuals participating in the General Assembly, and oversee it is meetings; and (h) informing the District Youth Council of initiatives of any matter that may affect youth development.

provisions.title: Ward Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: There is established a Ward Youth Council Secretariat for the purposes of assisting the Ward Youth Council in exercising of its functions.

provisions.title: Composition of Ward Youth Secretariat
provisions.content: The Ward Youth Council Secretariat shall be composed of five members as follows: (a) the Secretary who shall be a youth appointed by the Ward Executive Officer; (b) representative from learning institutions nominated by a meeting of Youth representatives from all learning institutions or student leadership in that particular District; (c) one youth with disability representing organizations of persons with disabilities within that particular ward; and (d) Two other members to be elected by the Ward Youth Council.

provisions.title: Functions of Ward Youth Council Secretariat
provisions.content: A Ward Youth Council Secretariat shall perform the following functions: (a) coordinate community events pertaining to youth issues at Ward level; (b) furnishing Ward Youth Council with reports on youth matters; (c) developing and maintaining relationships with individuals and organizations addressing issues of concern to youth at ward level; (d) promoting initiatives to support the well-being of youth at ward level; (e) coordinating the Ward Youth Assembly, maintaining the registry of individuals participating in the General Assembly, maintaining the registry of individuals participating in the General Assembly, and oversee it is meetings; and (f) informing the Ward Youth Council of initiatives of any matter that may affect youth development. Part IV – The role of local authority

provisions.title: Role of the local authorities at Regional level
provisions.content: The role of local authorities with respect to a Youth Organization at district level shall be to: (a) facilitate the execution of all youth councils activities at Regional level; (b) assisting the Minister to review or modify the youth policy, action Plan and their implementation after being adopted (c) ensure access to information and support regular communication between youth and Council ; (d) raise community awareness on issues related to the Regional Youth Council and issues affecting youth; (e) advocate for issues concerning youth and advice relevant Authorities accordingly; (f) provide youth with leadership opportunities including building their capacity in leadership, communication, team work, civic matters, solving problems, public speaking and networking; and (g) encourage youth to access Council services and facilities by making them user friendly.

provisions.title: Role of local authority at district level
provisions.content: The role of local authorities with respect to an organization at district level shall be to: (a) encourage youth to access the services and facilities offered by the organization by making them user friendly; (b) assist the Minister in reviewing, modifying and implementation of the National Youth Development Policy, strategies and plans; (c) ensure access to information and ensuring regular communication between youth and Council ; (d) raise community awareness about the District Youth Council and issues affecting youth; (e) advocate for issues concerning youth and advice relevant Authorities; (f) provide youth with leadership opportunities including building their capacity in leadership, communication, team work, civic affairs, solving problems, public speaking, and networking; and (g) facilitate of execution of all Youth Council activities at District levels.

provisions.title: Role of local authority at Ward level
provisions.content: The role of Local Authority with respect to a Youth Organization at Ward level shall be to: (a) encourage youth to access the services and facilities offered by the organization by making them user friendly; (b) assisting the Minister in reviewing, modifying and implementation of the National Youth Development Policy, strategies and plans; (c) ensure access to information and ensuring regular communication between youth and Council ; (d) raise community awareness about the Ward Youth Council and issues affecting youth; (e) advocate for issues concerning youth and advice relevant Authorities; and (f) provide youth with leadership opportunities including building their capacity in leadership, communication, team work, civic affairs, solving problems, public speaking, and networking; and (g) facilitate execution of all Youth Council activities at Ward levels. Part V – Election procedures

provisions.title: Election Supervisory Committee
provisions.content: (1) The elections of the Council shall be regulated by an Election Supervisory Committee which shall be appointed by the Minister . (2) The election Supervisory committee shall be composed of: (a) three officers from the Ministry for the time being responsible for matters relating to youth; (b) one officer from the Ministry responsible for matters relating to gender, children and community development; (c) one officer from the Ministry responsible for Regional Administration and Local Authorities; (d) three other persons with relevant knowledge who shall be appointed by the Minister from the Public Service; and (e) a law officer representing the Office of the Attorney General. (3) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1) , the Committee shall have the power to form subcommittees for purpose of regulating election at District and Ward level as it deems necessary. (4) The Election Supervisory Committee shall set procedures for nomination of members of the Council by considering among other things; youth population, wards, Districts, special groups and gender equality.

provisions.title: Functions of Election Supervisory Committee
provisions.content: The functions of an Election Supervisory Committee shall be to: (a) oversee the elections of members of National Regional District and Ward Youth Councils other than those appointed by virtue of their office; (b) verify the register of voters; (c) authenticate the candidates; (d) secure the ballots and ballot boxes; (e) identify the voting centre; (f) supervise the polling; (g) supervise the counting of votes; (h) issue certificates to the elected youth representatives at the respective level; (i) handle election related grievances; and (j) provide necessary facilities and assistance to ensure that persons with disabilities participate in the elections.

provisions.title: Voting procedure
provisions.content: (1) Voting at a polling station shall be conducted in a secret ballot and in one day. (2) The votes casted may be counted at the respective polling station and the results thereof shall be announced by an Authorized officer within fourty eight hours.

provisions.title: Election of National, Regional, District or Ward Youth Council Chairman
provisions.content: (1) A person who wishes to contest for the position of the National, Regional, District or Ward Youth Council Chairman shall make an application in the Form set out in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (2) The Election Supervisory Committee shall: (a) scrutinize the applications for the purpose of short listing three persons (b) present the names shortlisted under paragraph (a) to the General Assembly for the purpose of conducting election for Chairman.

provisions.title: Nomination of National Council Executive Secretary
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall invite applications from qualified youth for the position of Executive Secretary. (2) Upon receipt of application in sub regulation (1) , the Council shall scrutinize the applications, interview the selected candidates and send the names of three persons to the Minister for appointment.

provisions.title: Members of Council Advisory Board
provisions.content: (1) The General Assembly shall invite applications from qualified youth by virtue of their offices to apply for membership of the Council Advisory Board. (2) Upon receipt of application in subregulation (1) , the General Assembly shall scrutinize the application, interview the selected candidates and send names of five persons for the approval of the Minister. Part VI – General provision

provisions.title: Complaints
provisions.content: Where a person is aggrieved by decision made under these Regulations, he may, within fourteen days from the date of such decision, lodge his complaints to the Executive Secretary for determination.

provisions.title: Appeal
provisions.content: Where a person is not satisfied with the decision made by an Executive Secretary, he may, within fourteen days, appeal to the Minister for the determination which shall be final and conclusive.
